name: Tour around the Embalse de Iznájar — circular from Rute
name_original: Vuelta al Embalse de Iznájar — circular desde Rute
region_intro: Touring cycling around Rute offers routes through diverse landscapes in the province of Cordoba, Andalusia. The region is characterized by extensive olive groves, the Sierra de Rute mountains, and the large Iznájar Reservoir. Cyclists can explore varied terrain, from challenging climbs in the Sierra de Rute to scenic paths around the reservoir, often bordering the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park. Best touring cycling routes around Rute The most popular touring cycling route is Tour around the Embalse de Iznájar — circular from Rute, a 24.2 miles (39.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours to complete. This route features significant elevation changes around the Iznájar Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of Pico Bermejo – Puerto del Cerezo loop from Rute, a difficult 28.6 miles (46.0 km) path. This challenging path includes substantial climbing with views towards Pico Bermejo. Local touring cyclists also love the Lucena – Church of Santo Domingo loop from Rute, a 32.0 miles (51.5 km) trail leading through the surrounding countryside, often completed in about 3 hours 23 minutes. Touring cycling around Rute is defined by olive groves, mountain ascents, and reservoir views. The network offers options for different ability levels, including difficult routes with significant elevation. The routes in Rute are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 180 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Rute's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Rute?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes in the Rute area, offering a variety of experiences. The majority, more than 20, are considered difficult, with a few moderate and easy options available.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Rute?
A: The most pleasant time for touring cycling in Rute is generally between late September and early May. This period avoids the scorching summer heat, providing more comfortable conditions for exploring the region's olive groves, mountains, and reservoir views.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes in Rute?
A: While many routes in Rute are challenging, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. For families or beginners, it's advisable to check the route details for elevation and distance to ensure it matches your group's ability. The region's varied terrain means some paths through olive groves might be more gentle than mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Rute?
A: Touring cycling routes around Rute feature diverse terrain. You'll encounter extensive olive groves, challenging climbs in the Sierra de Rute mountains, and scenic paths around the large Iznájar Reservoir. Many routes also border the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park, offering varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Rute?
A: Yes, several touring cycling routes in Rute are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027043383/tour-around-the-embalse-de-iznajar-circular-from-rute" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around the Embalse de Iznájar — circular from Rute&lt;/a&gt; offers significant elevation changes around the reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31563060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pico Bermejo – Puerto del Cerezo loop from Rute&lt;/a&gt;, which includes substantial climbing.

Q: What natural sights can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass by significant natural features. You can explore areas near the &lt;strong&gt;Iznájar Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt;, the largest artificial lake in Andalusia, offering stunning views. The Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park is also nearby, home to the beautiful karst spring &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4050527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Río&lt;/a&gt; with its landscaped gardens and waterfalls. You might also encounter mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4415888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Cerezo&lt;/a&gt; or even views towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3256975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de la Tiñosa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there historical or cultural attractions to visit while cycling in Rute?
A: Absolutely. Rute is known for its unique cultural attractions. You can visit museums dedicated to local gastronomy, such as the Museo del Anis, Museo del Azucar (Sugar Museum), or Museo del Turrón. Historically, you might encounter ruins like Rute el Viejo or explore nearby castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/884528" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iznájar Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5343273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carcabuey Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy about Rute?
A: The touring cycling routes around Rute are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain climbs to scenic reservoir views and rides through picturesque olive groves. The variety caters to different skill levels, making it a popular destination for exploration.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging touring cycling routes in Rute for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Rute offers several challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1284813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lucena – Church of Santo Domingo loop from Rute&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 32-mile trail with significant elevation. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31816397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rute – Carcabuey Castle loop from Rute&lt;/a&gt;, which also features substantial climbing.

Q: Can I find viewpoints or scenic spots on the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer excellent viewpoints. The area around the Iznájar Reservoir provides stunning vistas, especially from elevated points like the Torre del Canuto. Routes through the Sierra de Rute mountains also offer panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes, including olive groves and distant peaks.

Q: Are there any routes that focus on the Iznájar Reservoir?
A: Yes, the Iznájar Reservoir is a prominent feature in many routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027043383/tour-around-the-embalse-de-iznajar-circular-from-rute" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around the Embalse de Iznájar — circular from Rute&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering a challenging ride with significant elevation changes around the lake. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10301175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rute – Iznájar Dam and Reservoir loop from Rute&lt;/a&gt;, which also explores this scenic area.
tips: From Puente Fernández we can see the Genil River that feeds the Iznájar Reservoir and part of the old Molinillo bridge, which is covered when the water level is high.
A good viewpoint of the Iznájar reservoir from the dam on the Genil River that feeds it. It is the largest reservoir in Andalusia. Its construction finished in 1969 and lasted a decade.
It is a town famous for its anise distilleries. There is even the Anís Monument and the Anís Museum.
